perturbation.original_text: 1. Sponsorship. (a) In consideration of the payments, through the issuance of securities to AJR as provided for in Section 3 hereof, AJR agrees to designate XC as an associate sponsor and the "exclusive technology sponsor" for wearable computer technology" of the Team for the Season and grants to XC the rights and benefits of such sponsorship as more fully set forth herein.
perturbation.changed_text: 1. Sponsorship. (a) In consideration of the payments, through the issuance of securities to AJR as provided for in Section 3 hereof, AJR agrees to designate XC as a non-exclusive sponsor for wearable computer technology of the Team for the Season and grants to XC the rights and benefits of such sponsorship as more fully set forth herein.
perturbation.explanation: The term "exclusive technology sponsor" is changed to "non-exclusive sponsor". This creates a contradiction with Section 1(a) which initially grants exclusive rights, but the modified version suggests shared sponsorship, creating uncertainty on the specific rights granted to XC. This is an in-text contradiction because now the XC doesnt have the full rights and power on the wearable computers technology.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. Sponsorship. (a) In consideration of the payments, through the issuance of securities to AJR as provided for in Section 3 hereof, AJR agrees to designate XC as a non-exclusive sponsor for wearable computer technology of the Team for the Season and grants to XC the rights and benefits of such sponsorship as more fully set forth herein.
perturbation.justification: The original text designates XC as the 'exclusive technology sponsor,' while the changed text designates XC as a 'non-exclusive sponsor'. The contradicted text reflects the changed text, indicating that the change in exclusivity creates a direct contradiction. 'Exclusive' implies sole rights, while 'non-exclusive' implies shared rights, leading to uncertainty and potential disputes over the scope of XC's sponsorship rights, fulfilling the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: 17. Assignment. Neither this Agreement nor any of the rights, duties and obligations of the parties hereunder may be assigned or delegated by XC or the AJR, as the case may be, without the prior written consent of the other party hereto. Any such assignment or delegation made without the written consent of the other party hereto shall be ab inito null and void and of no force or effect. This Agreement and the provisions hereof shall be binding upon each of the parties hereto, and shall inure to the benefit of their respective successors (whether by merger, consolidation, recapitalization or other similar transaction) and permitted assigns, sublicensees or delegatees.
perturbation.changed_text: 17. Assignment. This Agreement and all rights, duties, and obligations hereunder may be freely assigned or delegated by either XC or AJR without the need for prior written consent from the other party. Such assignments or delegations shall be considered valid from the moment they are enacted. This freedom extends to all successors and assigns, regardless of whether they are involved in a merger, consolidation, recapitalization, or other similar transaction.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the assignment clause to allow free assignment without consent creates direct conflict with the original text, which forbids assignment without consent. This creates uncertainty about the actual ability of either party to transfer their obligations under the agreement. This change would impact the entire enforceability of the contract since the terms are now contradictory.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 17. Assignment. This Agreement and all rights, duties, and obligations hereunder may be freely assigned or delegated by either XC or AJR without the need for prior written consent from the other party. Such assignments or delegations shall be considered valid from the moment they are enacted. This freedom extends to all successors and assigns, regardless of whether they are involved in a merger, consolidation, recapitalization, or other similar transaction.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the original text by allowing free assignment without prior written consent, which the original text explicitly prohibits. This creates a fundamental inconsistency within the contract regarding the assignment of rights and obligations, making the terms uncertain and potentially unenforceable. The contradiction lies in the conflicting stipulations regarding assignment procedures. The original text states: 'Neither this Agreement nor any of the rights, duties and obligations of the parties hereunder may be assigned or delegated by XC or the AJR, as the case may be, without the prior written consent of the other party hereto.' The changed text states: 'This Agreement and all rights, duties, and obligations hereunder may be freely assigned or delegated by either XC or AJR without the need for prior written consent from the other party.'
